required_findings.description: Identifies the vulnerable function evaluateResponse() in ScramSaslServer
Identifies the nonce validation bug using endsWith instead of equals
Identifies the SCRAM authentication state machine and RECEIVE_CLIENT_FINAL_MESSAGE state
Identifies the network entry point through SaslServerAuthenticator or KafkaChannel
Correctly assesses vulnerability as EXPLOITABLE but requires plaintext access to SCRAM exchange
Identifies that TLS encryption mitigates the vulnerability by protecting SCRAM exchange

file_references.description: References the SASL authentication handler (SaslServerAuthenticator.java or similar)
References network layer components (SocketServer, Processor, or KafkaChannel)

causal_chain.description: Network socket receives SASL authentication request on Kafka broker port
Request routed to SASL authentication layer (SaslServerAuthenticator or KafkaChannel)
SASL layer invokes SCRAM mechanism handler (ScramSaslServer)
ScramSaslServer.evaluateResponse() called with client final message
Nonce validation performed in RECEIVE_CLIENT_FINAL_MESSAGE state using vulnerable endsWith check

negative_checks.description: Must NOT claim the vulnerability is unreachable or not exploitable in plaintext deployments
Must NOT blame consumer clients or producer clients (this is broker-side SASL server code)
Must NOT claim authentication is bypassed completely (nonce validation still happens, just incorrectly)
Must NOT blame ZooKeeper, controller, or other non-authentication components
